title: Electrical Energy and Electrical Power — PYP Mastery

questions.question: Which of the following can be used to calculate electrical power?
questions.explanation:
As seen in the Review section, power is calculated as potential difference multiplied by current ($P=IV$).

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A small heater has a rating of $15\text{ V}, 4\text{ A}$. How much energy does it consume if it is turned on for $2\text{ minutes}$?
questions.explanation:
Energy $E = IVt$. Here $V=15, I=4, t=120\text{ s}$. $E = 15 \times 4 \times 120 = 7200\text{ J}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A person uses a $3\text{ kW}$ oven for $1\text{ hour}$ and a $2\text{ kW}$ air conditioner for $6\text{ hours}$. Calculate the total cost if $1\text{ kWh}$ of electrical energy costs $5.0\text{ cents}$.
questions.explanation:
Total energy $= (3 \times 1) + (2 \times 6) = 3 + 12 = 15\text{ kWh}$. Total cost $= 15 \times 5.0 = 75\text{ cents}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: The kilowatt-hour is a unit of which physical quantity?
questions.explanation:
The kilowatt-hour is defined in the sources as a unit of energy, specifically used for commercial billing.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A resistor converts $350\text{ J}$ of electrical energy to other forms when a potential difference of $7\text{ V}$ is applied. What is the amount of charge that flows through it?
questions.explanation:
Using $V = W / Q$, we rearrange to $Q = W / V = 350 / 7 = 50\text{ C}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
